description: Wikipedia article summary for U.S. census — genealogically central general topic

query.description: The topic to search for on Wikipedia

response.title: United States census
response.extract: The United States census is a decennial census mandated by Article I, Section 2 of the United States Constitution, which states: 'Representatives and direct Taxes shall be apportioned among the several States… according to their respective Numbers.' The first census was conducted in 1790, and a new census has been taken every ten years since. The U.S. Census Bureau publishes population data after each census and releases the underlying records 72 years later under the 72-Year Rule. Genealogists rely on U.S. census records — particularly the 1790-1950 surveys — for establishing residence, household composition, age, occupation, and immigration origin.
